sources.notes: Hard version of counting partial-permutation completions that are riffle shuffles.
Official editorial derives fixed/middle regions, handles the sorted-valid case by 2^len-len-1 over gaps, and restricts k ranges for crossing gaps to obtain linear total work.

annotation.summary: Count completions of a partial permutation that are riffle shuffles, under hard constraints requiring near-linear processing.
annotation.constraints: n is large; the O(n^2) split enumeration from F1 must be compressed to O(n).
annotation.core_idea: Use structural properties of riffle shuffles. If p_i<i then value p_i must be on the left side of the split, and if p_i>i it must be on the right. A valid shuffle has fixed prefix/suffix regions and a middle non-fixed region, so most known entries determine the side of their gaps. If all known entries are fixed, each -1 run contributes the closed form 2^len-len-1 plus the sorted case. Otherwise, for crossing gaps left->right or right->left, each split k demands a unique count of left-side elements inside the gap; this restricts k to an interval. Intersect all intervals, precompute same-side gap contributions, and only enumerate the reduced k range and crossing gaps, whose product is O(n).
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case after factorial/power precomputation.
tricks: infer side from p_i compared with i
closed form for all-unknown sorted-valid runs
restrict split k by crossing gaps

solution_variants.name: gap-range-dp-combinatorics
solution_variants.summary: Classify gaps by endpoint sides, compute same-side factors once, and enumerate only feasible split values constrained by crossing gaps.

solution_variants.name: quadratic-split-count
solution_variants.summary: The easy-version enumeration remains a useful conceptual baseline but does not pass the hard version.

tags.evidence: The task is counting completions of a partial permutation under a riffle-shuffle constraint.
The hard solution compresses gap contributions and feasible split ranges into reusable count states rather than enumerating every split/gap pair.
Binomial coefficients and closed forms over unknown runs are central to the count.
The decisive observation is the fixed-prefix/fixed-suffix/middle structure of riffle shuffles.
